title: Network Delay Time
description: You are given a network of n nodes, labeled from 1 to n. You are also given times, a list of travel times as directed edges times[i] = (ui, vi, wi), where ui is the source node, vi is the target node, and wi is the time it takes for a signal to travel from source to target. We will send a signal from a given node k. Return the minimum time it takes for all the n nodes to receive the signal. If it is impossible for all the n nodes to receive the signal, return -1.
constraints: All the pairs (ui, vi) are unique
thinking_questions: Shortest time to reach all nodes = single-source shortest path from k. Which algorithm?
Dijkstra's uses a min-heap to always process the closest unvisited node. How does it work?
After finding shortest paths to all nodes, what do you return?

hints.L1: Single-source shortest paths from node k. Use Dijkstra's algorithm. The answer is the maximum shortest path to any node.
hints.L2: Dijkstra's: min-heap (time, node). Process each node once. Relax edges: if current_time + edge_weight < known_time, update.

follow_up_questions: Why does Dijkstra's not work with negative edge weights?
What algorithm would you use if edges could be negative? What's its complexity?
Why return max(dist.values()) rather than the time to a specific node?
solution_notes: Dijkstra's from k. min-heap, skip if already processed. Return max shortest path. -1 if not all reached. O(E log V).
